title: reidia tetrandra
intro: Reidia tetrandra, also known as the Indian tinospora or giloe, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. The medicinal properties of Reidia tetrandra are attributed to its roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Reidia tetrandra is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, and powders, and is also used as a component in Ayurvedic and Unani medicines.
